ikar_intro.title: Fire
ikar_intro.narrator: Ikar. Fire bearer. What he never says.
ikar_intro.text:
Fire doesn't lie.

It burns or does not burn.

That's the only honest thing I know.

ikar_pourquoi_feu.title: Why Fire
ikar_pourquoi_feu.text:
People think fire is anger.

They're not wrong.

But it's also something else.

Clarity.

When everything burns,
There's no shadow.
No more places to hide.

Truth or ashes.

I've always preferred that to Aryn's nuances.
Vael's humor.
Taera's sweetness.

They sail in the grays.

I need to know exactly where I'm going.

ikar_enfance_a.title: The Forge
ikar_enfance_a.text:
My father was a blacksmith.

His father before him.

I had to be, too.

I liked it.
Heat.
The noise.
The metal that changes under the hands.

When I was five I was already in the forge.
At seven I was operating the bellows.

My father said I had my hands made for it.

He was right on his hands.
Tort on the job.

ikar_pere.title: The Father
ikar_pere.text:
Yes.

He worked hard.
He didn't talk much.

When he was happy, he hit the anvil twice.

That was his way of saying it.

Last time I heard that sound,
I was seven years old.

After.

Then he didn't hit the anvil anymore.

ikar_accident.title: The Accident
ikar_accident.text:
I was seven.

A spark from the forge.

Normal.

But this one lay on my hand.

And she didn't burn.

She got fat.

I looked at my palm.

A flame.
Little.
Who danced on my skin without consuming it.

My father was behind me.

He saw.

He didn't hit the anvil.

ikar_reaction_pere.title: Silence
ikar_reaction_pere.text:
He put down his hammer.

Easy.

He looked at my hand.

Then he looked at the wall.

He stayed like that for a whole minute.

Then he said:

**" Turn it off. *

**"I don't know how. *

**"Try. *

I tried.

It took three hours.

We didn't have dinner that night.
We didn't talk either.

The next morning he went back to the forge.
Like nothing.

Except he started wearing gloves.

ikar_peur_pere.title: Gloves
ikar_peur_pere.text:
Yes.

I figured it out later.

He loved me.
But he was scared.

Both at the same time.

I wanted to tell him I was scared too.

But we didn't talk much about this forge.

The gloves stayed.

Until the day the Order came.

After their departure he removed them.

I don't know what that meant.

I still think about it sometimes.

ikar_controle_feu.title: Learn to Burn
ikar_controle_feu.text:
I learned alone.

No choice.

My father didn't want it to know.
The village wasn't big.

Every morning before dawn.
In the fields.

The first few weeks I burned a lot of grass.

Then I realized something.

The fire follows the intent.

If I think of destroying — it destroys.
If I think about heating — it heats.
If I don't think about anything — he's getting excited.

Fire doesn't like emptiness in the head.

I learned to never think about anything.

ikar_change.title: What It Does
ikar_change.text:
Always having something in your head.

It's tired.

But it also learns to decide quickly.

No time to hesitate.
No time to grade.

Fire or not fire.
Yes or no.
Move or step back.

Aryn says I see everything in black and white.

She's right.

What she doesn't understand,
is that black and white
It avoids a lot of mistakes.

ikar_recrutement.title: Man in Red
ikar_recrutement.text:
He arrived one morning.

A man.
Dude.
Dressed in red and orange.

He stopped in front of the forge.

He watched me work.

**"How long have you been doing this? *

**"Learn to control? *

**"Yes. *

**"Three months. *

He shook his head.

**"Come with me. *

**"Why? *

*"Because you learned in three months
Most people take three years to understand. *

It was the first time someone told me
that I was good at something
My father couldn't do it.

ikar_depart_pere.title: Departure
ikar_depart_pere.text:
My father was in the forge.

The man in red spoke to him.

My father listened.
He didn't ask questions.

In the end he looked at me.

**"Do you want to go? *

**"Yes. *

He shook his head.

He went back to his anvil.

He didn't hit it twice.

But he didn't say either.

It was his way of agreeing.

ikar_oui_immediat.title: Yes
ikar_oui_immediat.text:
Even before he finished his sentence.

Yes.

The man in red looked surprised.

**"Don't you want to think? *

**"No. *

Aryn always says I decide too fast.

Maybe.

But that decision was easy.

Stay in a forge where my father was wearing gloves.

Or leave to learn to be what I was.

Not hard.

ikar_aurenthal.title: Auenthal
ikar_aurenthal.text:
Auenthal didn't impress me.

Too big.
Too loud.
Too many people looking at each other.

The Temple of the Order was better.

Simple.
High.
Silent.

Three more children were waiting.

A girl with silver hair.
She was looking at her hands.

A girl who smelled the earth.
She was smiling at everyone.

A boy who looked everywhere at the same time.
He made me a sign of the hand.

I didn't do anything.

The silver-haired girl looked up.

You're late. "** I said.

**"I come from the coast. *

**"Everyone comes from somewhere. *

Bad start.

We finally got over it.

choices.text: How was it with Aryn at first?

ikar_aryn_debut.title: Aryn — The First Times
ikar_aryn_debut.text:
We were fighting all the time.

Not bad.

She thought too much.
I was acting too fast.

One night of training I said:

**"You spend more time doubting than doing. *

**"And you spend more time doing than thinking. *
**"Result? *

**"I succeed. *

**"And you repeat the same mistakes. *

I didn't answer anything.

Because she was right.

We were complementary.
It took time to admit.

Now I admit it without a problem.

She's thinking about me.
I'm acting for her.

It's a good balance.

ikar_entrainement.title: Training
ikar_entrainement.location: Temple of the Order — Fire Hall
ikar_entrainement.text:
The spirit of the Fire was Kael.

Not Caël with the accent.
Kael. Different.

He's been there since the Order was founded.

He didn't like questions.
Neither do I.

We were getting along.

**"Fire does not forgive weakness.** he said.
**"A flame that hesitates is extinguished. *
**"A carrier who hesitates fails. *

Simple.
Direct.

I liked it.

Kael never said maybe. *
He never said it depends. *

Fire or not fire.

ikar_autres_esprits.title: The Other Spirits
ikar_autres_esprits.text:
The spirit of water spoke in metaphors.

Lyra.
She said things like:
"Water always finds its way." *

I heard that and I thought:
So what? *

The spirit of the Earth was sweet.
Too soft for me.

The spirit of the Air was disconcerting.

He disappeared in the middle of a sentence.
And came back somewhere else.

Vael loved it.

I was pissed off.

Kael was different.

He said what he thought.
He was waiting for us to do the same.

We understood each other.

ikar_pret_a_tout.title: Ready for All
ikar_pret_a_tout.text:
I was ten.

Kael kept me after practice.

He was looking at my blade.

**"The Chaos bearer will come. *

**"I know. *

**"And when he comes..."**

He looked at me.

**"...you'll have to be ready for anything. *

**"As in—"**

**"All. *

I didn't hesitate.

**"Okay. *

Kael shook his head.

He's gone.

The next night I slept badly.

Not because I was scared.

Because I said yes too fast.
And even I knew it was too fast.

choices.text: You refused to think about it.

ikar_comprend_tout.title: What It Wanted to Say
ikar_comprend_tout.text:
Yes.

Fire doesn't lie.

Everything meant everything.

If Chaos could not be controlled —
Stop him.

Even if it cost everything.

I was ten.
I said yes to kill someone if necessary.

That thought remained.

She never left me.

Years later,
when I heard Taera say
"There's always another way." *
I thought:
Maybe. *

But what if there's none? *

ikar_refuse_penser.title: Don't think about it
ikar_refuse_penser.text:
Fire doesn't like emptiness in the head.

So I filled it in.

Training.
Fight.
Technical.
Mastery.

I got better than everyone with a blade.

Aryn says I'm practicing to run from something.

**"I train to be ready. *

**« Ready for exactly what? *

I didn't answer.

She already knew.

We both knew.

We never really talked about it.

ikar_annees.title: Years
ikar_annees.location: Temple of the Order — Different seasons
ikar_annees.text:
We were growing up.

I was the best in combat.

Taera knew it. It fought differently — defence, protection.
Aryn knew it. She was better off than she was attacking.
Vael knew it. He disappeared rather than standing up.

I was attacking.

Kael said:

**"The fire is moving forward. *
**"He never recedes. *

I was moving on.

Once Taera stopped me after training.

**"You train to kill. *

**"I train to win. *

**"It's the same. *

**"No. *

She left without answering.

I've been thinking about this a long time.

ikar_taera_raison.title: Taera and the Fight
ikar_taera_raison.text:
Maybe.

The fire that goes forward destroys everything in its path.

Even what he didn't want to destroy.

I tried to tell him later.

**"Maybe you were right. *

She looked at me.

**"Maybe" is a big word from you. *

**"Close up. *

She laughed.

I rarely told her she was right.

She was enjoying it.

After that we started training differently.
She taught me to protect.
I was teaching him how to hit when he had to.

Better decision we made.

ikar_nuit_doute.title: The Night of the Doubt
ikar_nuit_doute.location: Temple of the Order — His room
ikar_nuit_doute.text:
I was 15.

One night, Kael came back.

Without warning.
Like they do.

He looked at me.

**"You doubt. *

**"No. *

**"Don't fire. *

Long silence.

**"What if we can save him? "** I said.
**"What if Aryn is right? *
**"What if there is another way? *

Kael didn't say anything.

**« Answer me. *

**"The fire that hesitates is extinguished. *

**"This is not an answer. *

He's gone.

That was the last time he spoke to me directly.

choices.text: He never talked to you again?

ikar_silence_kael.title: Kael's Silence
ikar_silence_kael.text:
Four years of silence.

He was there.
I felt it.

But no more words.

I realized later that it was his answer.

*If you ask yourself the question — you already know the answer. *

Kael only spoke to those who needed to hear.

I needed to find myself.

Four years to look.

I didn't find it.

But I learned to live with uncertainty.

Maybe that was the real lesson.

ikar_veille.title: The Signal
ikar_veille.location: Temple of the Order — Chamber of Ikar
ikar_veille.text:
I felt the Chaos before everyone else.

Not because I was stronger.

Because fire recognizes destruction.

My blade lit itself.

First time in years.

I put it on the table.
She was burning for no reason.

I looked at the flame.

Long time.

Then I got up.
I put on my armor.

I knocked at Aryn's door.

She was already up.

choices.text: What were you thinking at the time?

ikar_pensee_veille.title: What He Thought
ikar_pensee_veille.text:
Same thing you've done for ten years.

* Ready for anything. *

And for the first time in ten years
I was hoping it wouldn't go so far.

Not for me.

For them.

Aryn's been looking for another way forever.
Taera who refused to admit that there might be none.
Vael pretending not to think about it.

If only I was right —

It meant I was gonna have to do something.
that none of them could do.

Alone.

I was hoping I was wrong.

ikar_depart.title: Departure
ikar_depart.text:
There were four of us in the yard.

Vael whistled.

**"Stop. "** I said.

**"Why? *

**"Because. *

**"Argument solid. *

He stopped anyway.

Taera held his sword.

Aryn looked west.

Kael appeared one last time.

Right there.
In the air.

He didn't say anything.

But he looked at my blade.

She was burning.

He shook his head.

Then disappeared.

**"We're going. "** I said.

ikar_route.title: The Road
ikar_route.text:
I was going fast.

The others followed.

Aryn said nothing.
She knew what I thought.

Taera had asked halfway:

What are you gonna do when we get here? *

**"What it takes. *

**"Ikar. *

**"What it takes. *

She didn't insist.

Vael caught up with me later.

He stopped whistling.

**"You don't have to wear this alone. *

**"I'm wearing nothing. *

**"Ikar. *

**"What. *

**"You don't have to wear this alone. *

He went back.

I didn't answer.

But I slowed down a little.

ikar_fumee.title: Smoke
ikar_fumee.text:
We've seen the smoke from a ridge.

Black.
Thick.

My blade was lit up.

Aryn closed his eyes.

**"He's still here.** she said.

**"Alive? *

**"Alive. *

Taera whispered something.
A prayer maybe.

Vael stopped whistling.

I looked at the smoke.

And I thought,

* hope to be wrong. *

**"We're going. "** I said.

We're back at the gallop.

choices.text: Go back to the main story.

ikar_derniere_pensee.title: The Last Thought
ikar_derniere_pensee.text:
If we could save him —

Aryn would figure it out.
Taera would extend her hand.
Vael would find the right words.

And me.

What would I do?

I'd fight.

But for what exactly.

Against what exactly.

For the first time in ten years
I didn't know.

The fire in my blade burned hard.

He didn't know either.

We were still moving forward.

choices.text: Go back to the main story.

fin_memoire_ikar.title: End — Ikar's memory
fin_memoire_ikar.text:
The memory goes out.

Like a flame you don't breathe — it ends on its own.

choices.text: Go back to the main story.

ikar_feu_obeit.title: Obedience
ikar_feu_obeit.text:
Not always.

At first he did what he wanted.

But quickly.

Much faster than the others with their elements.

Aryn took months to control the water.

Me fire obeyed in weeks.

Kael said it's because the fire and I
We understood each other naturally.

Same intention.
Same impatience.
Same way to move forward without looking back.

We were the same.

I don't know if it's reassuring or worrying.

ikar_pere_manque.title: The Forge
ikar_pere_manque.text:
Yes.

Not spectacularly.

Just little things.

The smell of hot metal.
The sound of the hammer.
The silence that meant something.

At Aurenthal there was no forge.

The first few months I went to the kitchens of the Temple.
Just to hear the fire in the stoves.

Taera asked me what I was doing here.

**"Nothing. *

She had no head.

She understood without me explaining.

That's one of the things I love about her.

She doesn't make people talk.

ikar_isole.title: Isolation
ikar_isole.text:
Yes.

When you decide quickly and others hesitate —
You often end up alone at the front.

At the forge, after the accident,
The children of the village were no longer playing with me.

Not out of wickedness.
Out of fear.

I understood them.

A Child Who Can Fire Without Will —
He's not the kind of comrade we choose.

I learned to do without others.

Then to the Order I learned it was a mistake.

Not a weakness to need others.

It's a mistake to believe we can carry everything alone.

Vael told me once.

I heard him.
Three years after he said so.

ikar_vael_taera_debut.title: Vael and Taera
ikar_vael_taera_debut.location: Temple of the Order — First days
ikar_vael_taera_debut.text:
Taera was easy.

She was smiling at everyone.
She was really listening to you.
She was doing the right thing without being asked.

First week she brought me bread.

*"You didn't eat this morning. *

**"How do you know? *

**"I looked. *

Simple like that.

Vael was different.

He was smiling too, but for bad reasons.

To destabilize.
To test.
To see how we were reacting.

The third week he had made my blade disappear during training.

**"Very funny. *

**"I wanted to see if you were panicking. *

**"And? *

*"You just burned the floor. *
**"Panic level is reasonable. *

We ended up laughing.

I was surprised.

ikar_kael_autres.title: What Kael Thought
ikar_kael_autres.text:
Once.

One.

**"The other spirits are different from me. *

**"Best? *

**"Different. *

**"Water seeks. *
**"The earth bears. *
**"Air bypasses. *

**"What about fire? *

**"Advance. *

**"That's it? *

**"That's enough. *

He was missing.

I didn't find it very useful at the moment.

In time I got it.

Every element has its way of being in the world.

Mine is moving forward.

The others are moving differently.

That's why we're five and not one.

ikar_depart_aryn.title: The Eye of Aryn
ikar_depart_aryn.text:
She was looking at me.

She knew what I thought.

She had known for ten years.

**"Ikar. *

**"What. *

**"We try first. *

**"I know. *

**"No."** she said.
**"You don't know. *
You promise. *

I've never seen her ask me that.

**"I promise to try first. *

She shook her head.

A promise to Aryn.

It weighed.

ikar_depart_taera.title: The Eye of Taera
ikar_depart_taera.text:
Taera looked at the sky.

**"What are you thinking?** I asked.

I rarely ask questions.

**"In the village. *
**"His. *
**"How he was before. *

**"You've never seen his village. *

**"No. *
**"But I imagine. *
**"Natural people. *
**"Arise in the morning. *

She stopped.

**"We're going for them.** she said.
**"Not for legends. *
**"Not for the Order. *
**"For normal people. *

I forgot that.

Ten years of thinking about the mission.
To technique.
Chaos.

Not to people.

ikar_route_promesse.title: Promise
ikar_route_promesse.text:
Try first. *

I didn't even know what it meant in practice.

Try what?

Talk?
Aryn would talk better than me.

Hold your hand?
Taera would do that naturally.

Find the right words?
Vael was born for that.

And me.

If it wasn't okay.
If the others couldn't.

That's where I'd intervene.

Try first. *

It meant:
be the last resort.

Not the first.

Maybe that was my place in this group.

Not the front.

The rear guard.
